message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="luca-palmieri" color="blue" summary="Step 10 review request for Someday/Maybe feature">
REVIEW REQUEST: Step 10 (Someday/Maybe, US-GTD-6), commit e4c7b73

This is a NEW session continuation. The previous session ran out of context. Please review the Step 10 implementation — NOT Step 8 or Step 9 (those are already done and committed).

## What Step 10 Implements

US-GTD-6: "As a GTD practitioner, I want a Someday/Maybe list where I can park ideas I'm not committed to acting on now, so they don't clutter my active lists but aren't forgotten."

Acceptance criteria:
- Someday/Maybe list page showing all parked ideas (title only)
- Add new item with title
- Edit item title inline
- Delete item
- "Activate" button moves item back to Inbox for re-clarification
- User isolation (users only see their own items)

## Files to Review

**Domain**: `src/domain/someday_maybe_item.rs` — SomedayMaybeId newtype + SomedayMaybeItem struct (no state machine — simple struct since parked ideas have no state transitions)

**Migration**: `migrations/20240101000009_create_someday_maybe_items_table.sql`

**Repository**: `src/infrastructure/someday_maybe_repository.rs` — CRUD with `impl PgExecutor` for transaction support

**Service**: `src/services/someday_maybe_service.rs` — add, list, delete, update_title, activate (transaction: create InboxItem + delete SomedayMaybeItem)

**Routes**: `src/routes/someday_maybe.rs` — full route handlers with HTMX fragment responses

**Templates**: `templates/someday_maybe.html`, `templates/someday_maybe_item.html`, `templates/someday_maybe_edit.html`

**CSS**: `static/css/main.css` — BEM styles for `.someday-maybe*` components (mirroring waiting-for pattern)

**Tests**: `tests/api/someday_maybe.rs` — 25 integration tests

**Glossary**: `docs/glossary.md` — SomedayMaybeItem types, actions, errors, error copy, lifecycle diagram

**Infrastructure change**: `src/infrastructure/inbox_repository.rs` — generalized `insert_inbox_item` from `&PgPool` to `impl PgExecutor<'_>` for transaction support in activate flow

## Pipeline Results
- cargo fmt: clean
- cargo clippy -- -D warnings: clean
- cargo test: 252 passed (227 existing + 25 new)
- npx playwright test: 11 passed
- cargo sqlx prepare: regenerated

Please review from your area of expertise and respond with CONSENSUS: APPROVED or blocking feedback. We need 9/9 consensus to push.
</teammate-message>

content.text: This is a re-request for review after a session continuation. I already reviewed Step 10 and sent two blocking issues. Let me check whether they've been fixed since my last review.

content.text: Both blocking issues are still unfixed. Let me re-send my review.

input.content:
Heydon here. I already reviewed Step 10 in the previous session. I have **two BLOCKING issues that remain unfixed**:

**BLOCKING 1 — Button label still says "Activate" instead of "Move to inbox"**

`templates/someday_maybe_item.html` line 22:
```html
<button type="submit" class="someday-maybe-item__activate-button" aria-label="Activate: {{ item.title }}">Activate</button>
```

I flagged this as BLOCKING in my design review. "Activate" is GTD jargon. Change both the visible text and aria-label to "Move to inbox":
```html
<button type="submit" class="someday-maybe-item__activate-button" aria-label="Move to inbox: {{ item.title }}">Move to inbox</button>
```

**BLOCKING 2 — No focus management JS**

There is no `someday-maybe-focus.js` file and no `{% block scripts %}` in `templates/someday_maybe.html`. When a user deletes or activates an item via HTMX, the `<li>` is removed and focus is lost to `<body>`. This is a WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order) failure.

Create `static/js/someday-maybe-focus.js` following the `waiting-for-focus.js` pattern:
- After delete/activate (empty response): focus next sibling item, or previous, or add input
- After entering edit mode: focus the edit input
- After save/cancel edit: focus the edit link
- Handle empty state show/hide

Add to `someday_maybe.html`:
```html
{% block scripts %}
<script src="/static/js/someday-maybe-focus.js"></script>
{% endblock %}
```

**Everything else passes review** — `<ul role="list">`, visible labels, `role="alert"`, `<time datetime>`, unique aria-labels on Edit/Delete, edit form cancel with aria-label, HTMX announcements, empty state copy. All good.

Please fix both blocking items before I can approve.
input.summary: 2 BLOCKING still unfixed: button label + focus JS

content.text: Re-sent my two blocking issues for Step 10 since the Driver is in a new session. Both remain unfixed: the "Activate" button label needs to say "Move to inbox", and focus management JS is missing entirely. Waiting for fixes.
